revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「'''車持部'''（くるまもちべ、くらもちべ）は古代日本の[[部民]]のひとつである。大王の輿輦の製作、管理などをつかさどった。その料地を所有し、管理した人々が[[伴造]]の[[車持氏|車持公]]であるとされている。 == 歴史 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[履中天皇]]5年条には、車持君が[[筑紫国]]の車持部をほしいままに検校し，さらに[[宗像神社]]に…」

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:車持部]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q111123775]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuramochibe''' (車持部, also pronounced Kurumamochibe) was one of the [[:en:Bemin|Be groups]] in ancient Japan. They were responsible for the production and maintenance of imperial palanquins. The individuals who managed and supervised these tasks, often owning the associated land, were members of the [[Kuramochi clan]] (車持氏), specifically the Kuramochi no Kimi.
== History == According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan), in the 5th year of [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richū]]'s reign, Kuramochi no Kimi appropriated the resources of the Kuramochibe in [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]] and seized the Kuramochibe allocated to the [[:en:Munakata Shrine|Munakata Shrine]]. As a punishment, the Kuramochibe in Tsukushi Province was confiscated.
== Provinces with Kuramochibe == Provinces where Kuramochibe were present, or are believed to have been present, include the following:<ref>Ōta Ryō, ''Seishi Kakei Jisho'' (Genealogical Dictionary of Surnames), Isobe Kōyōdō, 1920.</ref> * [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] * [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]] (''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' records, "Kuramochi no Kimi, descendants of Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto.") * [[:en:Iga Province|Iga Province]] (associated with Kuramochi no Omi) * [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]] (associated with [[Kuruma Otoshigami Shrine|Kuruma Shrine]]) * [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] (''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' mentions Kuramochi villages in Nagara District and Habu District. Kuramochi no Kimi is linked to the descendants of Isaza no Kimi.) * [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] (Okada District had a Kuramochi village; an inscribed earthenware from the Ikenoshita archaeological site in [[:en:Inzai|Inzai]] City reads: "Kuramochi □...second year of the □ calendar.") * [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]] (Makabe District contained a Kuramochi village.) * [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]] (The 1st-year tax ledger for Shika District mentions Kuramochi no Kimi Shimasu○; in the 2nd year, listed as Kuramochi no Kimi Shimasu○.) * [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] * [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] * [[:en:Tango Province|Tango Province]] * [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]] (Kuruma District in Gunma Prefecture was associated.) * [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa Province]] (Kuramochi village existed in Ōi District.) * [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]] * [[:en:Etchū Province|Etchū Province]] (Kuramochi District in Niikawa existed.) * [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]]
== Notes == {{Notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Kotobank}}
{{translated page|ja|車持部|version=94248420|comment=Imported full ja history}}

They were responsible for the production and maintenance of imperial palanquins. The individuals who managed and supervised these tasks, often owning the associated land, were members of the [[Kuramochi clan]] (車持氏), specifically the Kuramochi no Kimi.

== Provinces with Kuramochibe == Provinces where Kuramochibe were present, or are believed to have been present, include the following:<ref>Ōta Ryō, ''Seishi Kakei Jisho'' (Genealogical Dictionary of Surnames), Isobe Kōyōdō, 1920.</ref> * [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] * [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]] (''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' records, "Kuramochi no Kimi, descendants of Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto.") * [[:en:Iga Province|Iga Province]] (associated with Kuramochi no Omi) * [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]] (associated with [[Kuruma Otoshigami Shrine|Kuruma Shrine]]) * [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] (''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' mentions Kuramochi villages in Nagara District and Habu District. Kuramochi no Kimi is linked to the descendants of Isaza no Kimi.) * [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] (Okada District had a Kuramochi village; an inscribed earthenware from the Ikenoshita archaeological site in [[:en:Inzai|Inzai]] City reads: "Kuramochi □...second year of the □ calendar.") * [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]] (Makabe District contained a Kuramochi village.) * [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]] (The 1st-year tax ledger for Shika District mentions Kuramochi no Kimi Shimasu○; in the 2nd year, listed as Kuramochi no Kimi Shimasu○.) * [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] * [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] * [[:en:Tango Province|Tango Province]] * [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]] (Kuruma District in Gunma Prefecture was associated.) * [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa Province]] (Kuramochi village existed in Ōi District.) * [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]] * [[:en:Etchū Province|Etchū Province]] (Kuramochi District in Niikawa existed.) * [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]]

== Provinces with Kuramochibe == Provinces where Kuramochibe were present, or are believed to have been present, include the following:<ref>Ōta Ryō, ''Seishi Kakei Jisho'' (Genealogical Dictionary of Surnames), Isobe Kōyōdō, 1920.</ref> * [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] * [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]] (''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' records, "Kuramochi no Kimi, descendants of Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto.") * [[:en:Iga Province|Iga Province]] (associated with Kuramochi no Omi) * [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]] (associated with [[Kuruma Otoshigami Shrine|Kuruma Shrine]]) * [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] (''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' mentions Kuramochi villages in Nagara District and Habu District. Kuramochi no Kimi is linked to the descendants of Isaza no Kimi.) * [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] (Okada District had a Kuramochi village; an inscribed earthenware from the Ikenoshita archaeological site in [[:en:Inzai|Inzai]] City reads: "Kuramochi □...second year of the □ calendar.") * [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]] (Makabe District contained a Kuramochi village.) * [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]] (The 1st-year tax ledger for Shika District mentions Kuramochi no Kimi Shimasu○; in the 2nd year, listed as Kuramochi no Kimi Shimasu○.) * [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] * [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] * [[:en:Tango Province|Tango Province]] * [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]] (Kuruma District in Gunma Prefecture was associated.) * [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa Province]] (Kuramochi village existed in Ōi District.) * [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]] * [[:en:Etchū Province|Etchū Province]] (Kuramochi District in Niikawa existed.) * [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]]

== Provinces with Kuramochibe == Provinces where Kuramochibe were present, or are believed to have been present, include the following:<ref>Ōta Ryō, ''Seishi Kakei Jisho'' (Genealogical Dictionary of Surnames), Isobe Kōyōdō, 1920.</ref> * [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] * [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]] (''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' records, "Kuramochi no Kimi, descendants of Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto.") * [[:en:Iga Province|Iga Province]] (associated with Kuramochi no Omi) * [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]] (associated with [[Kuruma Otoshigami Shrine|Kuruma Shrine]]) * [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] (''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' mentions Kuramochi villages in Nagara District and Habu District. Kuramochi no Kimi is linked to the descendants of Isaza no Kimi.) * [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] (Okada District had a Kuramochi village; an inscribed earthenware from the Ikenoshita archaeological site in [[:en:Inzai|Inzai]] City reads: "Kuramochi □...second year of the □ calendar.") * [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]] (Makabe District contained a Kuramochi village.) * [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]] (The 1st-year tax ledger for Shika District mentions Kuramochi no Kimi Shimasu○; in the 2nd year, listed as Kuramochi no Kimi Shimasu○.) * [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] * [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] * [[:en:Tango Province|Tango Province]] * [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]] (Kuruma District in Gunma Prefecture was associated.) * [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa Province]] (Kuramochi village existed in Ōi District.) * [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]] * [[:en:Etchū Province|Etchū Province]] (Kuramochi District in Niikawa existed.) * [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]]
== Notes == {{Notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Kotobank}}

== Provinces with Kuramochibe == Provinces where Kuramochibe were present, or are believed to have been present, include the following:<ref>Ōta Ryō, ''Seishi Kakei Jisho'' (Genealogical Dictionary of Surnames), Isobe Kōyōdō, 1920.</ref> * {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} * {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province}} (''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' records, "Kuramochi no Kimi, descendants of Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto.") * {{ill|Iga Province|en|Iga Province|WD=Q1047128|lt=Iga Province}} (associated with Kuramochi no Omi) * {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province}} (associated with [[Kuruma Otoshigami Shrine|Kuruma Shrine]]) * {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province}} (''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' mentions Kuramochi villages in Nagara District and Habu District. Kuramochi no Kimi is linked to the descendants of Isaza no Kimi.) * {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province}} (Okada District had a Kuramochi village; an inscribed earthenware from the Ikenoshita archaeological site in {{ill|Inzai|en|Inzai|WD=Q637901|lt=Inzai}} City reads: "Kuramochi □...second year of the □ calendar.") * {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province}} (Makabe District contained a Kuramochi village.) * {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province}} (The 1st-year tax ledger for Shika District mentions Kuramochi no Kimi Shimasu○; in the 2nd year, listed as Kuramochi no Kimi Shimasu○.) * {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}} * {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province}} * {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|WD=Q1054331|lt=Tango Province}} * {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province}} (Kuruma District in Gunma Prefecture was associated.) * {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa Province}} (Kuramochi village existed in Ōi District.) * {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province}} * {{ill|Etchū Province|en|Etchū Province|WD=Q906899|lt=Etchū Province}} (Kuramochi District in Niikawa existed.) * {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|Tsukushi Province|WD=Q1206461|lt=Tsukushi Province}}

As a punishment, the Kuramochibe in Tsukushi Province was confiscated.

Kuramochi no Kimi is linked to the descendants of Isaza no Kimi.) * {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|qid=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province|1=Shimōsa Province}} (Okada District had a Kuramochi village; an inscribed earthenware from the Ikenoshita archaeological site in {{ill|Inzai|en|Inzai|qid=Q637901|lt=Inzai|1=Inzai}} City reads: "Kuramochi □...second year of the □ calendar.") * {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|qid=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province|1=Hitachi Province}} (Makabe District contained a Kuramochi village.) * {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|qid=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province|1=Ōmi Province}} (The 1st-year tax ledger for Shika District mentions Kuramochi no Kimi Shimasu○; in the 2nd year, listed as Kuramochi no Kimi Shimasu○.) * {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|qid=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}} * {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|qid=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province|1=Harima Province}} * {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|qid=Q1054331|lt=Tango Province|1=Tango Province}} * {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|qid=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province|1=Kōzuke Province}} (Kuruma District in Gunma Prefecture was associated.) * {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|qid=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa Province|1=Wakasa Province}} (Kuramochi village existed in Ōi District.) * {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|qid=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province|1=Echizen Province}} * {{ill|Etchū Province|en|Etchū Province|qid=Q906899|lt=Etchū Province|1=Etchū Province}} (Kuramochi District in Niikawa existed.) * {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|Tsukushi Province|qid=Q1206461|lt=Tsukushi Province|1=Tsukushi Province}}
